text:
Market today: Risk-off mood with inflation sticky above 4% and the Fed under political pressure. That combo keeps rate-cut hopes muted — bad for high-multiple tech and chip names, which is basically your whole book.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 15% and your largest position on margin; that loss is amplified and it needs a decision soon. PLTR is down 13% with the same margin concern — two big losers at once is real portfolio stress. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record; on margin, a sudden gap-down could hit hard before you react.

Watch: (1) Micron is sinking after a rough week — chip sentiment is fragile and that bleeds directly into AVGO and SMH. (2) Grantham calling this the most expensive market in American history is a sentiment warning; if institutions start rotating out, your concentrated tech book feels it first.

Bottom line: Two margin positions bleeding double-digits in a high-inflation, risk-off tape is your single biggest problem — AVGO and PLTR need a size or stop decision today.
